news.title: Experts wonder ‘Where is the CDC?’ as hantavirus outbreak unfolds
news.summary: The CDC is playing an unusually small, and quiet, role in responding to the hantavirus outbreak.

news.title: Opinion: RFK Jr. allegedly ‘collected’ a dead raccoon’s penis. Was it bioethically justifiable?
news.summary: There are scientific reasons to study raccoon reproductive anatomy. But did RFK Jr.’s actions fit within a bioethical framework?

news.title: Opinion: Dr. Glaucomflecken wants the corporatization of medicine to be national news
news.summary: “We want, we need to keep medicine local. I think that's good for patients, it's good for communities,” Dr. Glaucomflecken says on the “First Opinion Podcast.”

news.title: Advancing Frontline Care With a Quadruplet Regimen for Transplant-Ineligible Myeloma Patients
news.summary: Explore a quadruplet treatment regimen now approved for newly diagnosed multiple myeloma patients who are transplant ineligible.

news.title: Sanford Health unveils deal to integrate Minnesota-area North Memorial Health, invest $600M
news.summary: The deal, expected to close later this year, is another attempt from the major rural nonprofit system to enter the Minneapolis market. To do so, it plans a $600 million investment into the two-hospital North Memorial Health, which will help stabilize one financially strained safety-net facility and double the size of the other.

news.title: The ACA exchanges dominated Q1 earnings calls. Here's what payer, health system execs had to say
news.summary: Political tumult around the Affordable Care Act's exchanges colored the final months of 2025, and talk about what the marketplaces look like now dominated many insurer and provider earnings calls in Q1.

news.title: AI-augmented behavioral health provider Theris launches out of stealth
news.summary: As sessions take place, AI agents record and analyze sessions, with the goal of augmenting care and automating clinical workflows.

news.title: Trump plans to fire FDA chief Marty Makary: report
news.summary: President Donald Trump has approved a plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, M.D., according to a report from The Wall Street Journal.

news.title: How policy, reimbursement incentives, could help healthcare address its climate footprint
news.summary: Climate change poses clear risks to healthcare, a recently published Health Affairs analysis reads, making recommendations on decarbonization and climate resilience strategies.

news.title: Op-ed: It's time to make more strategic bets on AI in healthcare
news.summary: EY's John Ward outlines how healthcare leaders with limited investment resources make the right AI moves for near-term and long-term value.

news.title: Health Tech Weekly Rundown: Tether rolls out medical AI for phones, wearables; Medaptus launches operational ‘command center’
news.summary: Stay up to date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Amazon Pharmacy to offer home delivery for Novo Nordisk's Ozempic pill
news.summary: Amazon Pharmacy will make Novo Nordisk's Ozempic pill available for home delivery, the company announced Thursday.

news.title: Staffing firm Cross Country Healthcare to be acquired by Knox Lane for $437M
news.summary: The deal would bring the 40-year-old, publicly traded company under a growth-oriented investment firm with a prior background in healthcare staffing.

news.title: Clover Health's MA membership grows 51% year-over-year
news.summary: Clover Health's membership climbed 51% year-over-year in the first quarter, reaching 155,773, per its earnings report released Wednesday.

news.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.ai over AI chatbot allegedly presenting itself as licensed medical professional
news.summary: The state's suit alleges Character.ai violated the state's Medical Practice Act after an AI chatbot falsely claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist, even providing a fake license number.

news.title: MRD as a Decision Engine: Rethinking Oncology Trial Design from Early Phase to Registration
news.summary: As MRD shifts from prognostic marker to intervention trigger, sponsors must rethink trial design, endpoints and decision-making.

news.title: New Hepta blood test could predict GLP-1 response
news.summary: While GLP-1s like Ozempic and Wegovy are being widely prescribed for weight loss, doctors currently do not have a way to predict if a patient will successfully respond to the drugs.

news.title: Trump plans to fire FDA chief Marty Makary: report
news.summary: President Donald Trump has approved a plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, M.D., according to a report from The Wall Street Journal.

news.title: A protective gene sent to the liver cleared amyloid from mouse brains, sparking hope for an Alzheimer’s gene therapy
news.summary: The calling card of Alzheimer’s disease is a buildup of misfolded amyloid protein in the brain, but many medicines struggle to penetrate the barrier protecting the mind from outside interference. A new study suggests that crossing the blood-brain barrier might not be necessary at all to clear amyloid plaques—and that a one-time gene therapy could one day prove an effective treatment for the devastating neurodegenerative disease.

news.title: Analysts cut enGene cancer sales forecast after updated data widen gap to J&J
news.summary: Leerink Partners analysts slashed their sales forecast for enGene Therapeutics’ bladder cancer candidate after phase 2 data dented their belief that the biotech can match rivals, including Johnson & Johnson.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Biopharma veteran takes her CEO Cue
news.summary: Not long after the departure of former CEO Usman Azam, a new leader is taking her Cue in the form of Shao-Lee Lin, M.D., Ph.D. Lin steps into Cue’s president and chief exec spot after previously serving as founder and CEO of eye disease specialist Acelyrin, which later merged with Alumis.

news.title: Odyssey’s CEO sees $304M IPO as chance to create a ‘little large pharma’
news.summary: Odyssey Therapeutics has become the latest biotech to voyage to the public markets via an upsized $304 million IPO this morning.

news.title: Banyan BioInnovations puts down roots with $100M, Icon partnership
news.summary: Banyan BioInnovations is debuting with more than $100 million aimed at developing NewCos around promising clinical assets, plus a partnership with global contract research organization Icon.

news.title: BeOne shelves 5 cancer programs, halts phase 2 rheumatoid arthritis trial
news.summary: BeOne Medicines has shaken up its early pipeline, dropping five phase 1 cancer programs and stopping a phase 2 arthritis trial based on new data.

news.title: Antibodies that stop measles from stabbing cells could become new treatments for the surging virus, study suggests
news.summary: Though a measles vaccine has existed for more than 60 years, much still remains unknown about how exactly the shot generates protection against the virus. Now, scientists have turned to the immune systems of vaccinated people to dig up answers—and their findings may lead to new medicines against the resurgent disease.

news.title: FDA to reconsider shock rejection of cell therapy Ebvallo. Could uniQure be next?
news.summary: After a surprise rejection in January, the FDA has agreed to reconsider a T-cell therapy based on the same single-arm trial that the agency had previously found problematic. The case could reignite investors’ hope of a potential reversal for uniQure's high-profile gene therapy, too.

news.title: Blackstone invests $250M in Anagram to reduce burden of cystic fibrosis complication
news.summary: For people living with exocrine pancreatic insufficiency due to cystic fibrosis, the only treatment option is a regimen that can include up to 40 pills a day. With a $250 million investment from Blackstone Life Sciences, Anagram Therapeutics hopes to reduce that pill burden to just three pills daily.

news.title: 思派、镁信、圆心：曾经的“医药险”三巨头，如今走到命运分叉
news.summary: 行业从“医药险”的资本故事，回到商业基本面看盈利的阶段。

papers.title: LiVeAction: a Lightweight, Versatile, and Asymmetric Neural Codec Design for Real-time Operation
papers.summary: Modern sensors generate rich, high-fidelity data, yet applications operating on wearable or remote sensing devices remain constrained by bandwidth and power budgets. Standardized codecs such as JPEG and MPEG achieve efficient trade-offs between bitrate and perceptual quality but are designed for human perception, limiting their applicability to machine-perception tasks and non-traditional modalities such as spatial audio arrays, hyperspectral images, and 3D medical images. General-purpose compression schemes based on scalar quantization or resolution reduction are broadly applicable but fail t

papers.title: BioTool: A Comprehensive Tool-Calling Dataset for Enhancing Biomedical Capabilities of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Despite the success of large language models (LLMs) on general-purpose tasks, their performance in highly specialized domains such as biomedicine remains unsatisfactory. A key limitation is the inability of LLMs to effectively leverage biomedical tools, which clinical experts and biomedical researchers rely on extensively in daily workflows. While recent general-domain tool-calling datasets have substantially improved the capabilities of LLM agents, existing efforts in the biomedical domain largely rely on in-context learning and restrict models to a small set of tools. To address this gap, we
